title: MCLN-Case-8955973-Testing Document-06-23
fullText: Scenario-1- WM is now updating the misc_alpha_field_2 for the CIG item Before the fix: WM was not updating the misc_alpha_field_2 for some of the CIG items. After the fix: WM is now updating the misc_alpha_field_2 for the CIG items, and INT3 tasks are completing successfully with inventory updating correctly without any issues. Item-447367.00, Currently in Case pick location, we have Onhand-‘0’.   The order quantity is 30 for item 097154.00. If there isn't enough inventory in the case pick location, the system performs the base allocation and creates INT9, INT3, and INT50 tasks. We have now implemented a fix to update the AID with the required fields and to create the PLD and WMI records, similar to the custom process. ALLOC_INVN_DTL: Before task execution: After completing INT9 task: After completing INT3 task: Scenario-2: standard fill wave with a CIG item: R unning a standard fill wave with a CIG item, and the wave completed successfully without any issues. Scenario-3: WM is not stamping the sku_attr_1 in alloc_invn_dtl when allocation_type = STD. The wave is completing without any issues; But the INT50 allocation is moving to status 99. To confirm, I tested the same scenario in another lower environment where our changes are not present. There too, I observed the same behavior, INT50 allocation is in cancelled status. As per the flow document, INT50 allocations are not applicable for the replenishment wave.

chunks.text:
[Document: MCLN-Case-8955973-Testing Document-06-23]

Scenario-1- WM is now updating the misc_alpha_field_2 for the CIG item Before the fix: WM was not updating the misc_alpha_field_2 for some of the CIG items. After the fix: WM is now updating the misc_alpha_field_2 for the CIG items, and INT3 tasks are completing successfully with inventory updating correctly without any issues. Item-447367.00, Currently in Case pick location, we have Onhand-‘0’.   The order quantity is 30 for item 097154.00. If there isn't enough inventory in the case pick location, the system performs the base allocation and creates INT9, INT3, and INT50 tasks. We have now implemented a fix to update the AID with the required fields and to create the PLD and WMI records, similar to the custom process. ALLOC_INVN_DTL: Before task execution: After completing INT9 task: After completing INT3 task: Scenario-2: standard fill wave with a CIG item: R unning a standard fill wave with a CIG item, and the wave completed successfully without any issues. Scenario-3: WM is not stamping the sku_attr_1 in alloc_invn_dtl when allocation_type = STD. The wave is completing without any issues; But the INT50 allocation is moving to status 99. To confirm, I tested the same scenario in another lower environment where our changes are not present. There too, I observed the same behavior, INT50 allocation is in cancelled status. As per the flow document, INT50 allocations are not applicable for the replenishment wave.
